description: A cross-platform coffee-shop ordering app — browse the menu, filter by category, build a cart, and place orders. Demonstrates a responsive navigation shell (a NavigationView pane on desktop/web, a bottom TabBar on phones) whose content reflows to multiple columns on wide screens, MVUX page-models wired through Uno.Extensions Navigation with one shared cart/orders state, and a warm light/dark theme — from one Uno single project targeting iOS, Android, WebAssembly, and desktop.

messages.content:
Generate a production-ready modern Coffee Shop ordering app (home + ordering flow) with:
- mock state
- a warm, cozy UI (beige/coffee tones) that also has a proper dark theme
- adaptive navigation (Home, Menu, Cart, Orders): a side pane on desktop/web, a bottom bar on phones

Home screen:
- hero image carousel with a primary "Order Now" CTA
- Today's Specials (horizontal scrolling cards)
- category chips
- featured products (image, name, description, price, +Add button)
- a cart summary strip and a rich empty-cart state

Navigation:
- a Menu screen with a working category filter
- a Cart screen (line items, quantity controls, totals, Place Order)
- an Orders screen with order history and status

Include:
- full working code
- navigation between screens that shares one cart/orders state
- product/order lists that lay out in multiple columns on larger screens
- seeded sample items (Latte, Cappuccino, Croissant, Iced Matcha)
Here's your **BrewHouse** app! It's built and running in the preview.

suggestedPrompts: Add a product detail page with navigation
Add a search box to the menu
Show a running item count badge on the Cart tab

messages.content: Generate a production-ready modern Coffee Shop ordering app (home + ordering flow) with a warm cozy UI plus a dark theme, adaptive navigation (Home, Menu, Cart, Orders) that is a side pane on desktop/web and a bottom bar on phones, a category filter, a cart with quantity controls and Place Order, and an order-history screen. Share one cart/orders state across screens, reflow lists to multiple columns on wide screens, and seed sample items.

I've created a cross-platform Uno Platform app at BrewHouse/BrewHouse.csproj (a single project targeting iOS, Android, WebAssembly, and desktop). It uses MVUX, Uno.Extensions Navigation and Uno Toolkit on the Uno Simple theme, with a warm light/dark palette.

**Shell** — Presentation/Shell.xaml is the navigation root; Presentation/MainPage.xaml is a responsive shell that shows a NavigationView pane on wide layouts (desktop/web) and a bottom utu:TabBar on narrow ones (phone), switched with the Toolkit {utu:Responsive} markup extension (no AdaptiveTrigger/VisualState). Both drive one region-based content area (uen:Region.Attached) that Navigation injects the active page (Home / Menu / Cart / Orders) into; App.xaml defines a shared DefaultResponsiveLayout breakpoint.

**Pages & models (MVUX)** — HomePage, MenuPage, CartPage and OrdersPage each bind an MVUX page-Model (HomeModel / MenuModel / CartModel / OrdersModel — partial records), registered via ViewMap in App.xaml.cs (with ReactiveViewModelMappings, so Navigation injects the generated bindable model) and DI-constructed with the shared ICartService plus an INavigator for the cross-tab navigation commands (Order Now, View Cart, Browse Menu); MainPage has a MainModel for the cart-count badge and ProductDetail uses a DataViewMap. Commands are async methods on the Model (MVUX generates the ICommand — no RelayCommand). Menu, Orders and Home's featured products use an ItemsRepeater + UniformGridLayout so they reflow from one column on phones to 2-3 on desktop/web; item-template commands use {utu:AncestorBinding}/{utu:ItemsControlBinding}, with ElementName bindings to a named page element to reach the page Model for chrome outside the item templates.

**State & data (MVUX)** — a DI-singleton CartService (Presentation/Services/) owns the cart and order history as one shared IListState<CartItem> / IListState<OrderRecord>, injected into every page-Model, so adding to the cart on one screen updates the cart strip, Cart page, Orders page and the tab badge together — one shared reactive state, with no messaging or manual PropertyChanged. Pages derive read-only IFeed/IListFeed projections (totals, counts, and the menu's category+search filter via Feed.Combine — reactive, no Clear()+Add()). Presentation/MockData/CatalogData.cs holds the immutable seed catalogue, Models.cs holds the entities as immutable partial records with key equality, and design-time previews bind a static *PageMockData.Data record.

**Theming** — ThemeColors.xaml defines the cream/espresso (light) and warm dark-roast (dark) palettes as ThemeDictionaries, merged onto the Simple theme via SimpleToolkitTheme ColorOverrideSource and referenced everywhere with {ThemeResource}. View-models expose plain data (bools/flags) so visibility and brush choices stay in XAML — a single BoolToVisibility converter keeps those choices in XAML, and the order-status pills are visibility-toggled, theme-coloured Borders. The app icon and splash are generated from the SVGs in Assets/ by Uno.Resizetizer.

The app is built and running. The user may ask me to modify it.
